hints.After: dominant flat hand slides forward over non-dominant flat hand
hints.Again: non-dominant palm up flat, dominant curved hand arcs and taps into palm
hints.All: right open hand sweeps in wide arc around left hand
hints.Alone: index finger extended upright, moves in small repeated circles
hints.And: open spread hand draws right while fingers close together
hints.Ask: both hands together prayer-like, pull toward body
hints.Be: B-handshape touches lips then moves forward
hints.Beautiful: open hand circles face, fingers close into flat-O
hints.Before: both flat hands face down, dominant draws back toward body
hints.Best: B-hand at mouth moves up, transforms to A-hand (superlative of good)
hints.Better: B-hand at chin moves upward closing to A-handshape
hints.Busy: both B-hands wrists touching, rock back and forth
hints.Bye: open flat hand waves side to side (goodbye wave)
hints.Can: both S-fists palms down, move downward simultaneously
hints.Cannot: right index strikes down across left index finger
hints.Change: both A-hands cross at wrists then twist/swap
hints.College: non-dominant flat hand palm up, dominant flat hand circles above then lands
hints.Come: both curved index fingers beckon/pull toward body
hints.Computer: C-hand at temple or dominant C-hand moves up non-dominant arm
hints.Day: dominant arm horizontal, index up, arm lowers like sunset
hints.Distance: D-hands or thumb+index point and move outward showing far away
hints.Do: both C-hands palms down, move side to side in unison
hints.Do Not: D-hand + NOT, or index finger shakes side to side firmly
hints.Does Not: similar to DO NOT, shake of head with sign
hints.Eat: fingertips pinched (flat-O) tap toward mouth repeatedly
hints.Engineer: E-hand or fingerspell; sometimes E slides across forehead
hints.Fight: both S-fists cross and strike toward each other
hints.Finish: both open hands palms toward self, flick suddenly outward
hints.From: F-hand or index finger draws back toward body from starting point
hints.Go: both index fingers point forward, move away from body
hints.God: G-hand or B-hand arcs from forehead upward and out
hints.Gold: G-hand at ear/earlobe, then Y-hand shakes forward
hints.Good: flat hand at chin, moves forward and down onto non-dominant palm
hints.Great: both open hands move apart indicating large/great size
hints.Hand: dominant flat hand slides across back of non-dominant flat hand
hints.Hands: both flat hands, dominant slides across non-dominant, then switch
hints.Happy: flat hand brushes upward on chest repeatedly
hints.Hello: open hand near forehead/temple, moves outward (salute wave)
hints.Help: dominant A-fist on non-dominant open palm, both hands rise
hints.Her: flat hand pushes sideways (toward the person referred to) or HER possessive
hints.Here: both flat hands palms up, move in horizontal circles in front of body
hints.His: flat hand pushes forward toward male reference
hints.Home: pinched fingers touch cheek then temple (eat+sleep)
hints.How: both curved hands back-to-back, roll forward and upward
hints.Invent: index at forehead flicks upward (idea emerging)
hints.It: index finger points to an object or neutral space
hints.Keep: both K-hands, dominant K taps non-dominant K
hints.Language: both L-hands near each other, move outward to sides
hints.Laugh: L-hands at corners of mouth, pull back and up
hints.Learn: open hand on non-dominant palm, closes moving to forehead
hints.ME: index finger points to own chest/self
hints.More: both flat-O hands tap fingertips together repeatedly
hints.My: flat hand placed firmly on chest
hints.Name: both H-hands (index+middle), dominant H taps non-dominant H twice
hints.Next: open hand behind other open hand, flips over and forward
hints.Not: thumb under chin moves forward OR A-hand at chin moves forward
hints.Now: both Y-hands or bent hands drop straight down abruptly
hints.On: dominant flat hand places on top of non-dominant flat hand
hints.Our: curved hand at right shoulder sweeps to left shoulder
hints.Out: dominant hand inside non-dominant C-hand, pulls out and upward
hints.Right: correct = R-hand slides across; direction = point right
hints.Sad: both open hands at face drop slowly downward
hints.Safe: both S-hands cross at chest, then uncross outward
hints.See: V-hand from under eyes points outward toward object
hints.Self: A-hand or index bounces forward toward reference
hints.Sign: both index fingers pointing up, circle alternately
hints.Sing: dominant hand waves back and forth over non-dominant arm
hints.So: (conjunction) flat hands held side by side; or S-O fingerspelled
hints.Sound: index finger at ear, circles forward
hints.Study: dominant open hand wiggles fingers over non-dominant flat palm
hints.Talk: index finger at mouth alternately taps forward
hints.Television: T-V fingerspelled or TV sign with T and V handshapes
hints.Thank: same as THANK YOU â€” flat hand from chin out
hints.Thank You: flat hand at chin/lips moves forward toward other person
hints.That: Y-hand drops onto non-dominant flat palm
hints.They: index finger sweeps in arc toward the group
hints.This: Y-hand or index finger taps on non-dominant palm
hints.Those: index finger points and sweeps toward group of things
hints.Time: index finger taps wrist where watch would be
hints.To: dominant index moves to touch tip of non-dominant index
hints.Type: both hands in typing position, fingers move as if typing on keyboard
hints.Us: U-hand touches one shoulder then swings to touch other shoulder
hints.Walk: both flat hands alternately move forward like walking feet
hints.Wash: dominant A-hand rubs in circle over non-dominant A-hand
hints.Way: both W-hands (or open hands) move forward in parallel tracks
hints.We: index finger touches own chest then swings to touch the other person(s)
hints.Welcome: dominant flat hand sweeps inward toward body
hints.What: index finger waggles across fingers of other open hand
hints.When: index fingers circle each other then one lands on the other
hints.Where: index finger waggles back and forth side to side
hints.Which: both A-hands alternate up and down (choosing)
hints.Who: L-hand near lips, index makes small circle near chin
hints.Whole: dominant flat hand circles over non-dominant flat hand then lands
hints.Whose: W-hand near chin, transitions to S then forward (WHO + possessive)
hints.Why: W-hand at forehead pulls down changing to Y-handshape
hints.Will: flat hand at cheek/face, moves forward into space
hints.With: both A-fists come together side by side
hints.Without: both A-fists come together then separate opening to open hands
hints.Words: index finger and thumb pinch together, tap on non-dominant index finger
hints.Work: both S-fists, dominant wrist taps non-dominant wrist repeatedly
hints.World: both W-hands circle each other, dominant lands on top
hints.You: index finger points directly toward the other person
hints.Your: flat hand pushes forward toward the other person
hints.Yourself: A-hand or index bounces forward twice toward the other person
